evals.name: jsonplaceholder-multi-entity-nested
evals.prompt: I want to add a new data source to data-world. Pull data from the JSONPlaceholder API at https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com — I need the posts and the users loaded into DuckDB so I can model them with dbt later.
evals.expected_output: A new data-ingestion/jsonplaceholder/ package with ingestion/posts.py and ingestion/users.py (each exposing fetch_/load_), an ingest_jsonplaceholder.py entry point, and an ingest:jsonplaceholder Taskfile entry. Running it creates raw_jsonplaceholder.raw_jsonplaceholder_posts (100 rows) and raw_jsonplaceholder_users (10 rows), each with an ingested_at column. The users table flattens the nested address/company objects into scalar columns rather than storing raw JSON.

evals.name: pokeapi-pagination
evals.prompt: Create an ingestion pipeline for the PokéAPI. I want the full list of pokemon from https://pokeapi.co/api/v2/pokemon — just the name and the url for each one. Load it into DuckDB.
evals.expected_output: A data-ingestion/pokeapi/ package with ingestion/pokemon.py and an ingest_pokeapi.py entry point. fetch_pokemon follows the paginated variant (loops the `next` cursor / indexes payload['results']) so it pulls all ~1300 pokemon, not just the first page. Running it creates raw_pokeapi.raw_pokeapi_pokemon with > 1000 rows, columns name + url + ingested_at.

evals.name: incremental-comments-per-post
evals.prompt: Add an ingestion pipeline that pulls JSONPlaceholder comments into DuckDB, one post at a time. Fetch the comments for each post id from https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com/comments?postId=<id> for posts 1 through 10. Make it incremental — if I re-run it, posts that are already loaded should be skipped rather than re-fetched.
evals.expected_output: A data-ingestion/jsonplaceholder_comments/ (or comments under a jsonplaceholder package) pipeline using the incremental load strategy: a get_loaded_ids(conn) helper, delete-before-insert per post_id, and an entry point that skips post ids already present. First run loads comments for posts 1-10 (50 rows, 5 per post) into raw_*.*_comments with a post_id key + ingested_at. A second run skips all 10 and inserts no duplicates.
